text_prompt.raw: Compare memory-bandwidth-bound and compute-bound inference bottlenecks in low-bit LLM decoding. Keep the answer under five sentences.

decoded_text.prompt: Compare memory-bandwidth-bound and compute-bound inference bottlenecks in low-bit LLM decoding. Keep the answer under five sentences.
decoded_text.generated: Memory-bandwidth-bound bottlenecks occur when the GPU cannot fetch weights fast enough to keep the compute units saturated, which is common in low-bit quantization due to increased memory access frequency. In contrast, compute-bound bottlenecks happen when the hardware processing capacity is fully utilized before memory limits are reached, often seen when using smaller models or highly optimized kernels. Low-bit quantization typically exacerbates bandwidth issues by increasing the number of memory transactions required per token, whereas compute-bound scenarios are more sensitive to kernel efficiency and arithmetic intensity. Addressing bandwidth issues usually involves techniques like weight caching or tiling, while optimizing compute-bound systems focuses on
decoded_text.full:
Compare memory-bandwidth-bound and compute-bound inference bottlenecks in low-bit LLM decoding. Keep the answer under five sentences.

Memory-bandwidth-bound bottlenecks occur when the GPU cannot fetch weights fast enough to keep the compute units saturated, which is common in low-bit quantization due to increased memory access frequency. In contrast, compute-bound bottlenecks happen when the hardware processing capacity is fully utilized before memory limits are reached, often seen when using smaller models or highly optimized kernels. Low-bit quantization typically exacerbates bandwidth issues by increasing the number of memory transactions required per token, whereas compute-bound scenarios are more sensitive to kernel efficiency and arithmetic intensity. Addressing bandwidth issues usually involves techniques like weight caching or tiling, while optimizing compute-bound systems focuses on
